faqs.question: What was the average college PPG for players who made the NBA?
faqs.answer: Players who made the NBA averaged 14.8 PPG in their final college season, compared to 12.1 for NBA G-League players, 10.3 for Europe players, 9.5 for Other International players, and 4.8 for those with no pro career.

faqs.question: What was the average college RPG for each pro destination?
faqs.answer: In their final college season, NBA players averaged 5.9 RPG, NBA G-League players 4.7, Europe players 4.3, Other International players 4.1, and no-pro players 2.2.

faqs.question: What was the average college APG for each pro destination?
faqs.answer: In their final college season, NBA players averaged 2.6 APG, NBA G-League players 2.1, Europe players 1.8, Other International players 1.6, and no-pro players 0.9.

faqs.question: What is the average height for each pro destination?
faqs.answer: NBA players averaged 6-6, NBA G-League players 6-6, Europe players 6-6, Other International players 6-6, and no-pro players 6-4.

faqs.question: How many D1 players were analyzed?
faqs.answer: We analyzed 18,688 D1 players whose last college season was between 2014-15 and 2023-24. Of these, 816 (4.4%) reached the NBA, 1065 (5.7%) went to the NBA G-League, 2073 (11.1%) played in Europe, 3168 (17.0%) played in other international leagues, and 11566 (61.9%) had no recorded pro career.

faqs.question: What percentage of D1 players go pro?
faqs.answer: 38.1% of D1 players in our dataset went on to play some form of professional basketball. Only 4.4% made the NBA with real regular season playing time.

faqs.question: How many players transitioned between pro levels?
faqs.answer: Among NBA-first players: 236 transitioned to international leagues and 30 moved to the G-League. Among NBA G-League-first players: 48 made it to the NBA and 640 went international. Among Europe-first players: 25 made it to the NBA, 122 came to the G-League, and 1028 went to other international leagues. Among Other International-first players: 21 made it to the NBA, 158 came to the G-League, and 974 went to Europe.

faqs.question: Which schools produce the most NBA players?
faqs.answer: The top NBA-producing schools (2015-2025): Kentucky (41), Duke (40), Arizona (20), Kansas (19), Gonzaga (17).

faqs.question: What is the average pro career length by destination?
faqs.answer: NBA-first players averaged 5.6 pro seasons, NBA G-League-first players averaged 4.0 pro seasons, Europe-first players averaged 3.5 pro seasons, and Other International-first players averaged 3.0 pro seasons.

faqs.question: What percentage of NBA players have long careers (5+ years)?
faqs.answer: Of 816 players who reached the NBA, 252 (30.9%) had careers of 5 or more seasons. These long-career players averaged 15.1 PPG in their final college season, vs 14.4 for those who left the NBA in under 5 years.

faqs.question: How many NBA G-League players eventually made the NBA?
faqs.answer: Of 1065 players whose first pro destination was the NBA G-League, 48 (4.5% if gl_count > 0 else 0) eventually earned real NBA playing time. These players averaged 13.7 PPG in their final college season.

faqs.question: How many European players eventually made the NBA?
faqs.answer: Of 2073 players who first went to Europe, 25 (1.2%) eventually made it to the NBA. They averaged 14.2 PPG in their final college season.

faqs.question: How many Other International players eventually made the NBA?
faqs.answer: Of 3168 players who first went to other international leagues (non-Europe), 21 (0.7%) eventually made it to the NBA. They averaged 11.7 PPG in their final college season.

faqs.question: What positions are most common among NBA players?
What were the average steals per game in college by pro destination?
faqs.answer: NBA players averaged 1.1 SPG in their final college season, NBA G-League 1.0, Europe 0.8, Other International 0.8, and no-pro 0.5.

faqs.question: What were the average blocks per game in college by pro destination?
faqs.answer: NBA players averaged 0.8 BPG in their final college season, NBA G-League 0.5, Europe 0.5, Other International 0.4, and no-pro 0.2.

faqs.question: How many years did NBA players spend in college on average?
faqs.answer: NBA players averaged 3.0 NCAA seasons before going pro, compared to 3.7 for players with no pro career.

faqs.question: Which schools produce the most NBA G-League players?
faqs.answer: Top NBA G-League-producing schools: Georgetown (13), Texas (13), Washington (13), LSU (13), Villanova (12).

faqs.question: Which schools produce the most Europe-bound players?
faqs.answer: Top Europe-producing schools: Colorado State (15), Loyola Marymount (14), Gonzaga (13), Oklahoma State (13), UC Irvine (13).

faqs.question: Which schools produce the most Other International players?
faqs.answer: Top Other International-producing schools: New Mexico State (23), Tennessee State (22), Cal State Fullerton (21), Prairie View A&M (21), Hawaii (20).

faqs.question: What are the stats of players who never went pro?
faqs.answer: Players with no recorded pro career averaged 4.8 PPG, 2.2 RPG, 0.9 APG in their final college season. Their average height was 6-4. This group represents 61.9% of all D1 players.

faqs.question: What happens to NBA players who don't last 5 years?
faqs.answer: Of NBA players with shorter careers (<5 years): 236 transitioned to international basketball, 30 moved to the NBA G-League, and 298 left professional basketball entirely.

faqs.question: How many Europe players had long careers?
faqs.answer: Of 2073 Europe-first players, 48 (2.3%) had careers of 5+ international seasons, while 850 (41.0%) left pro basketball relatively quickly.

faqs.question: How many Other International players had long careers?
faqs.answer: Of 3168 Other International-first players, 146 (4.6%) had careers of 5+ international seasons, while 1869 (59.0%) left pro basketball relatively quickly.
